caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5418, not 541840, and includes PR, creative and display occupations that are not a literal media-representative workforce.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Programmatic substitution is excluded from this V estimate and addressed in capture and terminal demand.
inputs.pi_dist: No public source measures 541840 commission-rate changes after AI adoption.
inputs.pi_moat: Contract term, transferability and client concentration are target-specific and unobserved.
inputs.t50_years: BTOS is broad-business evidence, not a 541840 workflow survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is an all-business proxy, not an industry measurement.
inputs.historical_analogs: Not used to set V; used only to frame adoption and terminal uncertainty.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: S5 is a media-buying valuation proxy and does not report owner age for 541840.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: False means not documented in reopened sources, not that succession risk cannot exist at a target.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a complete private-deal search and does not measure historic consolidation.
inputs.buy_mult: There is no reopened 541840 transaction-comps dataset; S5 is a media-buying proxy, not an exact NAICS comparable.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a target-execution assumption rather than observed exit evidence; no multiple expansion is used in C or terminal demand.
inputs.pricing_structure: The observed source is a large incumbent, not a representative sample of all 541840 firms.

Census S1 opens as the NAICS search application without a rendered 541840 result, so the exact scope text is not directly visible at the cited URL; this is a citation-location caveat, while S2 independently supports the intermediary commission model and the disclosed scope uncertainty prevents a score-changing inference.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $300,671 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541840); class <5: 916 firms, avg revenue/firm $464,112 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $72,634 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 107 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,610,327 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $252,016 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 60 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,427,867 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $849,461 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 50 firms, avg revenue/firm $14,497,680 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,268,887 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $67,318,188 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $10,535,296 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 14 firms, avg revenue/firm $191,069,071 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,902,310 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 50

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI can automate research, proposal, traffic, billing and administrative tasks, while station/publisher and agency relationship selling remains human. The supplied 4-digit role proxy is reconstructed task-by-task and carries a contamination caveat.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541840 ($2,214,615,737) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541840 ($5,400,046,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, identity, digests, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. The weak 541840 thesis is directionally supported by the incumbent commission model, broad adoption evidence and programmatic-channel evidence. Extensive authored proxy and uncertainty caveats remain publication material, and the Census scope citation has a non-material location weakness; no finding is likely to reverse a factor, score or thesis.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — 541840 Media Representatives
sources.what_it_supports: Official scope: independent representatives selling media time or space for media owners; distinguishes 541840 from agencies and media buying.

sources.title: iHeartMedia 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Katz Media earns commissions from national spot and online advertising sold for represented radio and television clients; documents the incumbent commission model.

sources.title: Large Firms With at Least 20 Employees Biggest AI Users
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS broad-business AI-use results: 17–20% overall use in the December 2025–May 2026 period and lower use for the smallest firms.

sources.title: Proximic by Comscore Releases 2025 State of Programmatic Report
sources.what_it_supports: Reported CTV share of surveyed media budgets and reallocations from linear TV, supporting the programmatic/linear-transition risk.

sources.title: Media Buying Agency Valuation
sources.what_it_supports: Public marketplace proxy: well-run media-buying agencies typically 4.5–6.0x EBITDA on fee revenue, with lower values for weak contracts, concentration or missing programmatic capability.

sources.title: IAB/PwC Internet Advertising Revenue Report: Full Year 2025
sources.what_it_supports: IAB reports nearly $300 billion of U.S. digital advertising revenue in 2025 and 13.9% year-over-year growth; broad advertising-market context only.

title: Media Representatives
